name: io.github.amitshukla2308/ripple
title: Ripple
description: Codebase intelligence platform exposed as 15 MCP tools — precise search, graph navigation, blast-radius analysis, change prediction, PR review, activity-weighted re-ranking, criticality scoring, auto-generated guardrail docs, and AI-code provenance consumption. Built for AI coding agents working in large, long-lived codebases. Compliance-ready for EU AI Act (August 2026), SOX, and PCI-DSS audit evidence.

highlights: Blast Radius v2 with activity-weighted reranking (+322% recall@10)
AI-code provenance consumption (Git AI / Agent Blame / Tabnine)
Compliance-ready: EU AI Act (Aug 2026), SOX, PCI-DSS
io.github.amitshukla2308/ripple.status: READY — Path A picked by Amit 2026-04-17. remotes[] entry points to SSE-over-localhost. Submission awaits final Amit OK.
submission_blockers: Amit's OK on pushing this manifest to the official MCP Registry. Validation passes the 2025-10-17 schema; all required fields present (name, description, version). Remotes entry uses SSE transport type per schema, URL is localhost — registry docs should accept per-user-install endpoints but we'll verify with their publish API before going live.

documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.name: ARTIFACT_DIR
documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.description: Directory containing graph_with_summaries.json, vectors.lance, cochange_index.json, criticality_index.json, guardrails/

documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.name: EMBED_SERVER_URL
documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.description: URL of the embedding inference server

documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.name: HR_GUARD_PATH
documented_env_vars_moved_here_because_r.description: Path to the Guard prototype for pattern checks (optional)

io.github.amitshukla2308/ripple.license: Apache-2.0 (already at repo root LICENSE — 190 lines, verified 2026-04-17)
roadmap_to_submission: 1. Package HR as `pip install ripple` (pyproject.toml exists, need PyPI token)
2. Add `hr-serve` CLI entrypoint that starts both embed + mcp servers with one command
3. Ship a tiny 'demo_artifact/' in the pip package so first-run doesn't need build/ pipeline
4. Submit to official MCP Registry via the publish API at registry.modelcontextprotocol.io
